content.full_text:
Re
Farn Por-rrrcAt- PRACTTcES CouurssroN
428 J Street . Suite 620 . Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
November 18,2005
Vicki De Kay, RN
5 Alvares Court
Sacramento, CA 95833
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-05-158
Dear Ms. Kay:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the post-
governmental employment restrictions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act').I As your
request seeks general assistance and does not provide specific information about
particular qroceedings or appearances, we are treating your request as one for informal
assistance.2 This letter is solely based on the facts prisented to us in your request. The
Commission does not act as a finder of fact when issuing advice or informal assistance.
(In re Oglesby (1975) I FPPC Ops. 71.) Our advice is applicable only to the extent that
the facts provided to us are correct, and all of the material facts have been provided.
QUESTION
May you work privately as a nurse consultant to Indian health centers after
recently retiring as a nurse consultant for the Department of Health Services?
CONCLUSION
The Act does not prohibit you engaging in any private employment to perform
duties related to your past work for the Department of Health Services provided you do
not appear before, or communicate with, your former agenay, as discussed below.
FACTS
Prior State Employment: You state that you separated from state service on May
27 , 2005 and retired on July 3l , 2005 after 1 5 years in the Department of Health Services
I Government Code sections 81000 - gl}14. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18^997, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not proiide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Regulation 18329, subd. (c), copy enclosed.)
i"1..s:4


File No. I-05-158
Page No. 2
job title was Nurse Consultant ("NC"). You indicatethatunder your agency's conflict-
of-interest code, you were required to file Statements of Economic Interests (or Form
700's).
Your role as an NC was to travel statewide to Indian health centers in rural and
urban areas. You provided consultation and technical assistance to staff in a wide variety
of health-related subjects. You also conducted on-site funding evaluations lasting from2
to 4 days depending on the systems that you evaluated.
Potential Future Employment: Now that you have retired, you would like to
consult independently with these clinics. Specifically, you would like to offer required
training in areas such as CPR and bloodborne pathogens, as well as more complex
consultation. However, you feel strongly that to provide a clinic with undefined "inside
information" regarding the funding evaluation is unethical, and you do not intend to
engage in this practice. You state that there are also many other areas of need in such
clinics about which you would like to advise the clinics, e.g., recordkeeping, regulations,
board of directors training, credentialing of professional employees, departmental
policies and procedures, HIV testing, and personnel issues. In your most recent
telephone contact with our Commission counsel on or about November 4,2005,you
indicated that you might also engage in OSHA training, writing a nursing manual, setting
up a diabetic screening program, and generally "providing anything a nurse can do." You
also added that some of your contemplated work might involve contacting DHS for
information.
ANALYSIS
Public officials who leave state service are subject to two types ofpost-
governmental restrictions under the Act,' colloquially known as the "revolving door"
prohibitions.a we discuss your questions undeieach of these provisions.
a
Permanent Ban: The first restriction is the "permanent ban" prohibiting a
former state ernployee from "switching sides" and participating, for
compensation, in any specific proceeding involving the State of California or
assisting others in the proceeding if the proceeding is one in which the former
state employee participated while employed by the state. (See sections B74ol-
87402; Reg. 18741.1.)
3 In addition, section 87407 prohibits certain state and local ofErcials from making, participating in
making, or using their official position to influence decisions affecting persons with whom they are
negotiating emplo5zment, or has any arrangement conceming employment. (Section 874}7;regulation
18747.) However, since you have already left your state position, we need not advise regarding this
provision.
a A comprehensive discussion of the elements of the two laws is set forth in the attached "Leaving
Your State Job? Post-Employment Restrictions May Affect You" We have attached this document for
your information. we do not repeat this information in the body of this letter.


File No. I-05-158
Page No. 3
The permanent ban is a lifetime ban and applies to any judictal, quasi-judicial, or
other proceeding in which you participated while you served as a state administrative
official. "'Judicial, quasi-judiciai or other proceeding' means any proceeding,
application, request for a ruling or other determination, contract, claim, controversy,
investigation, charge, accusation, arrest or other particular matter involving a specific
party or parties in any court or state administrative agency . . . ." (Section 87a00(c).)
To apply the permanent ban to your situation, you would need to identiff the
proceedings in which you participated while employed by the state.s You indicate that
you provided consultation and technical assistance to staff in a wide variety of health-
related subjects. You also indicate that you conducted on-site funding evaluations lasting
ftom2 to 4 days depending on the systems that you evaluated. Since you have not
provided any detail regarding these actions, it is not possible to determine whether such
actions constitute 'Judicial, quasi-judicial, or other proceedings" in which you
participated. In addition, it is difficult from the general description of the wide variety of
services you contemplate on providing in private employment, whether such services
would affect any of the potential proceedings in which you participated. Therefore, we
cannot fuither advise you regarding the provisions of the permanent ban.
o One-Year Ban: The second restriction is the o'one-year ban" prohibiting a state
employee from communicating, for compensation, with his or her former agency
for the pu{pose of influencing certain administrative or legislative action or
influencing certain proceedings. (See section 87406; Reg. 1g746.1.)
You retired from state service on July 31, 2005. The one-ye ar ban will expire 12
months from your date of separation from employment with DHS. While in effect, the
one-year ban prohibits any formal or informal appearance or oral or written
communication with your former agency for the principal pu{pose of supporting,
promoting, influencing, modi$zing, opposing, delaying, or advancing the action or
proceeding.
Under regulation 18746.1, your former agency includes any agency you worked
for or represented. Yoq have provided no information regarding your DHS work other
than that: (1) you provided consultation and technical assistance to staff in a wide variety
of health-related subjects, and that (2) you also conducted on-site funding evaluations
lasting ftom2 to 4 days depending on the systems that you evaluated. Since you do not
indicate that you worked for or represented any agency other than DHS. Therefore, it
does not appear that the one-year ban would prohibit you from engaging in any formal or
informal appearance or oral or written communication with any agency, other than DHS,
s "'Participated' means to have taken part personally and substantially through decision, approval,
disapproval, formal written recommendation, rendering advice on a substantial basis,lnvestigation or use
of confidential information as an officer or employee, but excluding approval, disapproval oirendering of
legal advisory opinions to departrnental or agency staff which do not involv" u rp"iffi" parfy or parties.,,
(Section 87a00(d); see also Reg. 18741.1.)


File No. I-05-158
Page No. 4
for the principal purpose of supporting, promoting, influencing, modi&ing, opposing,
delaying, or advancing the action or proceeding.
An appearance or communication includes, but is not limited to, conversing by
telephone or in person, coffesponding in writing or by electronic transmission, attending
a meeting, and delivering or sending any communication. In contrast to the permanent
ban, which only applies to 'Judicial or quasi-judicial,, proceedings, the one-jear ban
applies to "any appearance or corlmunication made for the purpose of influincing
administrative or legislative action,6 or influencing any action or proceeding involving
the issuance, amendment, awarding, or revocation of a permit, license, grari,or contract,
or the sale or purchase of goods or property." Thus, if you are called upon to appear or
communicate with your former agency, the prohibition in section 87406 will apply.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
By: Andreas C. Rockas
Counsel, Legal Division
AR:rd
I:\Adviceltrs\05-158
6 Section 82037 defines "legislative action" as "the drafting, introduction, consideration,
modification, enactment or defeat of any bill, resolution, amendmerit, report, nomination or other matter by
the Legislature or by either house or any committee,.subcommittee, joint or select committee thereof, or by
a member or employee of the Legislature acting in his official "upuiity. 'Legislative action, also means the
action of the Governor in approving or vetoing any bill.,,

QUESTION: May you work privately as a nurse consultant to Indian health centers after
recently retiring as a nurse consultant for the Department of Health Services?

CONCLUSION: The Act does not prohibit you engaging in any private employment to perform
duties related to your past work for the Department of Health Services provided you do
not appear before, or communicate with, your former agenay, as discussed below.
